name: Simply the Best Chocolate Brownies2
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces unsweetened chocolate, broken into 1/2 ounce pieces
2 ounces semisweet chocolate, broken into 1/2 ounce pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter and flour a square baking pan.
2. Sift together dry ingredients: all-purpose flour, cocoa, baking powder, and salt.
3. Melt butter in a double boiler; add chocolate and melt.
4. Beat eggs, sugar, and vanilla together until smooth.
5. Stir the melted chocolate mixture into the egg mixture until well combined.
6. Gradually stir in the dry ingredients.
7. Add sour cream to the batter and mix until just combined.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
9. Bake at 325 degrees Fahrenheit for 40 minutes.
Variations:
Macadamia Chocolate Chunk
- Add 4 ounces semisweet chocolate (cut into 1/4 inch chunks) to the batter.
- Add another 4 ounces semisweet chocolate on top, along with 1 1/2 cups raw, unsalted macadamia nuts.
- Increase cooking time by 10-15 minutes.
Peanut-Raisin
- Add 1 1/2 cups raisins to the batter.
- Spread 1 1/2 cups unsalted peanuts on top.
- Increase cooking time by 10-15 minutes.
Hazelnut Brownie Cake
- Pour 1 recipe of the basic brownie into a 9-inch cake pan and bake as directed above.
- In a double boiler, melt semisweet chocolate into boiling cream to form a ganache.
- Combine 1 cup of ganache with 1 cup chopped, toasted hazelnuts.
- Cut the cake in half and spread the hazelnut-ganache mixture between the layers.
- Pour plain ganache over the cake as icing.
- Pipe chilled ganache stars onto the cake for decoration.
